card.intuition: Two types of page updates: Reflow (Layout) recalculates the position and size of elements; Repaint simply updates the look of elements without changing their space.
card.analogy: Renovating a house: Reflow is like moving a wall (everything around it must shift). Repaint is like painting a wall a different color (the furniture stays exactly where it is).
card.time_complexity: O(N) for Reflow; O(M) for Repaint (where M < N typically)
gotchas: Reflow is MUCH more expensive than Repaint because it triggers a chain reaction of geometry calculations
Reading certain properties in JS (like offsetHeight or getBoundingClientRect) forces a synchronous 'forced reflow' which kills performance

exercise.prompt: Identify which action causes a Reflow and which causes a Repaint.
hints: If it changes 'how much space' it takes or 'where' it is, it's Reflow
If it's just a visual layer update, it's Repaint
exercise.test_code:
assert actions["Changing background-color"].lower() == "repaint"
assert actions["Changing width"].lower() == "reflow"
print("PASS")
